claim: AI-driven supply-side productivity gains could be the most powerful structural force restoring negative correlation, potentially overriding fiscal dominance regardless of fiscal policy or central bank reaction functions within 3-5 years.
needs: Evidence that AI productivity gains are appearing in macro TFP aggregates rather than just corporate capex and deployment metrics. Historical validation that positive supply shocks can override fiscal dominance. Resolution of the Solow paradox timing question (electricity took 30+ years, internet 10-15 years). A cited model supporting the claim that AI would override fiscal dominance 'regardless of' policy.

claim: EUR/USD carry-adjusted appreciation functions as a 2-4 month leading indicator for stock-bond correlation regime shifts.
needs: Larger sample beyond 3 historical validations with acknowledged false-positive episodes (2011-12 eurozone crisis). Validation that the indicator works when EUR/USD is driven by trade balance shifts rather than portfolio flows. Current flat reading is consistent with muddled regime but not signaling.

claim: Passive investing (~50% of equity AUM) extends the correlation-regime equity repricing lag from the historical 18-36 months to 36-60 months.
needs: Empirical measurement of passive flow impact on correlation regime repricing speed beyond 'three data points stitched together with intuition.' A mechanism explaining the specific 36-60 month range. Analysis of whether passive flows concentrate the eventual repricing into a shorter, sharper event.

claim: Italy's BTP-FTSE MIB correlation trajectory provides a 12-18 month advanced preview of the US stock-bond correlation regime.
needs: Accounting for fundamental structural differences: Italy operates within a currency union (redenomination risk has no US equivalent), Italian equity market is bank-heavy and cyclical (opposite of US tech-heavy), ECB TPI backstop involves moral hazard from other eurozone members (no Fed analogue). Evidence that timeline parallels are causal rather than coincidental.

claim: Financial Conditions Index miscalibration is 75-150bp in policy-rate-equivalent terms due to AI equity rally loosening conditions through a non-monetary channel.
needs: Alternative proxy methodologies beyond crude equal-weight vs. cap-weight to narrow the 40-200bp uncertainty range. Independent validation of the specific AI equity rally contribution to FCI loosening. Reconciliation with the Goldman FCI methodology and official Fed financial conditions assessments.

claim: The 2006-07 FX regime is the most informative dollar-cycle analogue for predicting the current dollar and correlation trajectory.
needs: Accounting for the critical fiscal starting point difference (2% deficit and 62% debt/GDP in 2006-07 vs. 6-7% deficit and ~125% debt/GDP today), which could change timing and severity by a factor of 2-3x. Validation that the dimensional comparison methodology weights the right factors.

claim: BOJ normalization is the single largest underpriced exogenous shock to US term premium and global correlation regimes, potentially adding 30-180bp to US term premium over 12-18 months.
needs: Narrowing of the 6x magnitude range (30-180bp) which currently undermines precision. Evidence that Japanese institutional selling would materially exceed the gradual reduction already underway since 2022 with limited measurable impact. Substantiation of ranking as largest underpriced shock vs. tariff escalation, fiscal expansion, and recession risks.

claim: The geopolitical correlation ratchet creates a structurally positive correlation bias through asymmetric escalation-spike vs. de-escalation-normalization dynamics and permanent sunk fiscal commitments.
needs: Larger sample of true geopolitical correlation events beyond Russia-Ukraine. Methodology to isolate the geopolitical channel from concurrent monetary and fiscal dynamics. Quantification of the ratchet's cumulative correlation impact and whether it saturates at some level of geopolitical tension.
